Return a pass/fail verdict.` | 1) Compose JSON payload with `toolCalls`, `exchanges`, `user_prompts`, `trigger_phrases` 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` 3) Inspect rendered .md output for field preservation 4) Verify decision confidence matches input 5) Verify git_changed_file_count matches explicit count | All structured fields present in output, counts match, file-backed JSON stays on the structured path | Rendered memory file content, CLI exit code 0 | PASS if structured fields preserved and hardening fixes hold | Check input-normalizer mapping, collect-session-data count handling, and workflow routing for structured inputs |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6534:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:38:| 153-A | JSON mode structured summary hardening | Post-save quality review output verification | `Run generate-context.js --json with a fully-populated payload. Confirm the CLI prints a POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW block and that the block reports PASSED with 0 issues. Return a pass/fail verdict.` | 1) Compose a complete JSON payload with `sessionSummary`, `triggerPhrases`, `keyDecisions`, `importanceTier`, `contextType` 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` 3) Capture full stdout 4) Locate the `POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW` block 5) Verify it reads `PASSED` with 0 issues | `POST-SAVE QUALITY REVIEW -- PASSED` header visible in stdout, `issues: 0` or equivalent no-issue indicator | CLI stdout log showing REVIEW block | PASS if REVIEW block is present and reports 0 issues; FAIL if block is absent or reports any issues | Check `scripts/core/post-save-review.ts`, `scripts/core/workflow.ts`, and CLI response rendering in `scripts/memory/generate-context.ts` |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6535:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:39:| 153-B | JSON mode structured summary hardening | sessionSummary propagates to frontmatter title | `Run generate-context.js --json with sessionSummary set to a meaningful task title (not "Next Steps"). Confirm the rendered memory file's frontmatter title matches the sessionSummary and is not a generic fallback such as "Next Steps".` | 1) Set `sessionSummary` to a descriptive, non-generic string (e.g., "Refactor auth pipeline for token refresh") 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` 3) Read the rendered .md frontmatter 4) Assert `title` field matches provided sessionSummary | Frontmatter `title` equals the sessionSummary value; no occurrence of "Next Steps" as the title | Rendered .md frontmatter | PASS if title matches sessionSummary; FAIL if title is "Next Steps" or any other fallback | Inspect input-normalizer title derivation and frontmatter assembly in workflow.ts |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6536:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:40:| 153-C | JSON mode structured summary hardening | triggerPhrases propagate to frontmatter trigger_phrases | `Run generate-context.js --json with an explicit triggerPhrases array. Confirm the rendered memory file's frontmatter trigger_phrases matches the supplied array and contains no file-path fragments.` | 1) Set `triggerPhrases` to meaningful keyword phrases (e.g., ["auth refactor", "token refresh"]) 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` 3) Read the rendered .md frontmatter 4) Assert `trigger_phrases` matches the provided array 5) Assert no entry looks like a file path (contains `/` or `.ts`) | Frontmatter `trigger_phrases` contains only the supplied keyword phrases; no path fragments present | Rendered .md frontmatter | PASS if trigger_phrases matches payload; FAIL if path fragments appear or array is replaced by heuristic output | Inspect trigger-extractor heuristic fallback and input-normalizer trigger_phrases passthrough |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6537:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:41:| 153-D | JSON mode structured summary hardening | keyDecisions propagate to non-zero decision_count | `Run generate-context.js --json with a keyDecisions array containing at least 2 items. Confirm the rendered memory metadata decision_count is greater than 0 and matches the number of supplied decisions.` | 1) Set `keyDecisions` to an array of 2+ decision strings 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` 3) Read the rendered .md file, including the `## MEMORY METADATA` YAML block 4) Assert `decision_count` > 0 and equals `keyDecisions.length` | Memory metadata `decision_count` equals the number of items in the `keyDecisions` array | Rendered .md metadata block | PASS if decision_count > 0 and matches array length; FAIL if decision_count is 0 despite supplied decisions | Check collect-session-data decision counting, input-normalizer keyDecisions mapping, and metadata-block rendering |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6538:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:42:| 153-E | JSON mode structured summary hardening | importanceTier propagates to frontmatter importance_tier | `Run generate-context.js --json with importanceTier set to "important". Confirm the rendered memory file's frontmatter importance_tier matches "important" and is not overridden to "normal".` | 1) Set `importanceTier` to `"important"` 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` 3) Read the rendered .md frontmatter 4) Assert `importance_tier` equals `"important"` | Frontmatter `importance_tier` equals the payload value | Rendered .md frontmatter | PASS if importance_tier matches payload; FAIL if overridden to "normal" or another default | Inspect importance_tier assignment in workflow.ts and input-normalizer passthrough |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6539:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:43:| 153-F | JSON mode structured summary hardening | contextType propagates for the full documented valid enum | `Run generate-context.js --json with contextType set to documented valid values including "implementation", "review", "debugging", and "planning". Confirm each rendered memory file preserves the payload value in frontmatter context_type instead of falling back to heuristics.` | 1) Save separate JSON payloads using `contextType` values `"implementation"`, `"review"`, `"debugging"`, and `"planning"` 2) `node .opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/memory/generate-context.js --json '<payload>' <spec-folder>` for each case 3) Read each rendered .md frontmatter 4) Assert `context_type` matches the payload value every time | Frontmatter `context_type` equals the payload value for every documented valid test case | Rendered .md frontmatter across the saved files | PASS if every valid test case preserves its explicit context_type; FAIL if any valid value is rewritten or defaulted | Inspect contextType passthrough in input-normalizer, detectSessionCharacteristics in session-extractor, and frontmatter assembly in workflow.ts |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6540:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:44:| 153-G | JSON mode structured summary hardening | Contamination filter cleans hedging in sessionSummary | `Run generate-context.js --json with sessionSummary containing "I think this might be important" and recentContext containing "As an AI, I should note". Confirm the saved memory does not contain these hedging/meta-commentary phrases.` | 1) Compose payload with hedging text in sessionSummary and meta-commentary in recentContext 2) Run generate-context.js --json 3) Read saved memory file 4) grep for "I think" and "As an AI" 5) Assert neither phrase appears | Saved memory free of hedging and meta-commentary phrases | grep output showing zero matches | PASS if hedging phrases absent from saved memory; FAIL if any contamination survives | Check contamination-filter.ts pattern list and workflow.ts cleaning call sites |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6541:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:45:| 153-H | JSON mode structured summary hardening | Fast-path filesModified to FILES conversion | `Run generate-context.js --json with userPrompts AND filesModified in the same payload. Confirm the saved memory contains a populated files section matching the filesModified entries.` | 1) Compose payload with userPrompts (triggers fast-path) and filesModified=["src/foo.ts"] 2) Run generate-context.js --json 3) Read saved memory 4) Assert FILES section contains src/foo.ts | FILES section populated with filesModified entries | Rendered memory file | PASS if FILES contains all filesModified entries; FAIL if FILES is empty or missing | Check input-normalizer.ts fast-path filesModified conversion |
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6542:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/16--tooling-and-scripts/153-json-mode-hybrid-enrichment.md:46:| 153-I | JSON mode structured summary hardening | Unknown field warning for typos | `Run generate-context.js --json with a deliberately misspelled field name "sesionSummary". Confirm a console warning is emitted naming the unknown field.` | 1) Compose payload with sesionSummary (missing 's') 2) Run generate-context.js --json 3) Capture stderr/stdout 4) Assert WARN message contains "sesionSummary" | Console WARN naming the unknown field | CLI stdout/stderr | PASS if warning emitted; FAIL if typo passes silently | Check KNOWN_RAW_INPUT_FIELDS in input-normalizer.ts |

The response must include memory (resume context), codeGraph (status/ok/empty/error with counts), cocoIndex (available boolean with binary path), structuralContext (`status`, `summary`, `recommendedAction`, `sourceSurface`, plus freshness guidance), and hints array.

./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6593:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/264-query-intent-routing.md:10:This scenario validates Query-intent routing in memory_context.
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6594:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/264-query-intent-routing.md:16:- **Objective**: Verify that memory_context auto-routes queries based on the query-intent classifier. Structural queries (containing keywords like "calls", "imports", "callers", "function", "class") must route to the code graph backend. Semantic queries (containing keywords like "similar", "find examples", "how to") must route to the standard memory/CocoIndex pipeline. Hybrid queries must trigger both backends and merge results. The classifier confidence score and matched keywords must be available in the response metadata.
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/iterations/iteration-024.md:6595:/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/manual_testing_playbook/22--context-preservation-and-code-graph/264-query-intent-routing.md:20:- **Prompt**: `Validate 264 Query-intent routing. Send three queries to memory_context: (1) a structural query like "what functions call handleMemoryContext", (2) a semantic query like "find examples of error handling patterns", (3) a hybrid query like "find all validation functions and explain their approach". Confirm: (1) structural query returns code graph results, (2) semantic query returns memory/CocoIndex results, (3) hybrid query returns merged results from both backends.`

./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/domains/business/customer-experience/README.md:7:Modern customer experience (CX) has evolved from reactive problem-solving to proactive relationship management, leveraging data analytics, artificial intelligence, omnichannel communication platforms, and sophisticated feedback mechanisms to anticipate customer needs and exceed expectations. The field requires expertise in understanding customer psychology, managing complex support ecosystems, implementing service management frameworks, and measuring satisfaction through both quantitative metrics and qualitative insights.
./.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/002-babysitter-main/external/library/specializations/domains/business/customer-experience/README.md:130:**Primary Focus:** Analyzing customer data and feedback to generate actionable insights for improvement.

codex
I’ve got the Mex scoring/reporting evidence in hand, and the next step is narrowing the concrete test surfaces instead of reading more generally. I’m now locating the external repo’s own test coverage and our repo’s existing memory/search/graph tests so the recommendations can slot into real suites and not just a future wishlist.

   132	- **What it does**: Mex compresses all issues into `100 - 10/error - 3/warning - 1/info` and surfaces that as the main health signal.
   133	- **Why it matters**: Public needs evidence by subsystem. A single number hides whether the real problem is markdown integrity, retrieval quality, stale graph context, or save/index health.
   134	- **Recommendation**: reject
   135	- **Priority**: 7
   136	- **Effort score**: 2/10
   137	- **Impact**: medium
   138	- **Impact score**: 7/10
   139	
   140	## Assessment
   141	- **New information ratio**: 0.10
   142	- **Updated classification**: `adopt now` = static markdown integrity lane, simplified guided maintenance surface; `prototype later` = dual freshness metadata, optional scaffold-growth companion; `reject` = markdown-first memory replacement, score-first health; `NEW FEATURE` = `spec-kit doctor`
   143	- **Definitive recommendation**: do not import Mex wholesale. Transplant only the lexical integrity layer and the repair-loop DX, while keeping retrieval, graph, and durable memory authority inside Spec Kit Memory.
   144	- **Validation status**: strict validation for the phase folder passed with `Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, though the script emitted a sandbox temp-file warning during execution.
   145	- **Write status**: this session is read-only, so I could not update [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md), create `implementation-summary.md`, or persist the final synthesis into the phase folder.
   146	
   147	## Recommended Next Focus
   148	1. Specify a narrow integrity phase: `path`, `edge`, and `index-sync` checks only, reported by severity and kept separate from recall scoring.
   149	2. Design `spec-kit doctor` around existing primitives: `session_bootstrap -> memory_health -> memory_save(dryRun) -> optional repair brief -> recheck -> next hints`.
   150	3. Revisit broader command/dependency/script audits only after a provider-neutral inventory model exists for npm, shell, Python, MCP commands, wrappers, and spec docs.

   172	
   173	## Assessment
   174	- New information ratio: 0.16
   175	- Questions addressed: quarter sequencing, dependency ordering, where `spec-kit doctor` fits, whether freshness precedes or follows integrity, when scaffold growth becomes safe
   176	- Questions answered: Q1 = integrity lane plus small maintenance surface; Q2 = `spec-kit doctor` plus advisory freshness metadata; Q3 = optional scaffold-growth companion after integrity and doctor exist
   177	- Novelty justification: the mechanisms were already known by iteration 030, but this pass adds the missing dependency graph and milestone timing
   178	- Dependency chain: `integrity lane -> guided maintenance surface -> spec-kit doctor -> advisory freshness -> optional scaffold-growth companion`
   179	
   180	## Ruled Out
   181	- markdown-first memory replacement, because prior synthesis concluded it would regress hybrid retrieval and causal/context tooling
   182	- single blended drift score as the main health surface, because subsystem evidence matters more than one aggregate number
   183	- Q1 adoption of command/dependency/script-coverage audits, because iteration 030 already recommended waiting for a provider-neutral inventory model
   184	
   185	## Reflection
   186	- What worked: using the final synthesis in [research.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/research/research.md#L268592) as the roadmap anchor, then validating each quarter against primary Mex source files
   187	- What did not work: the phase artifacts are noisy and partially duplicated, CocoIndex calls canceled during this pass, and the read-only sandbox prevented persisting this iteration into the phase folder
   188	- What I would do differently: turn the Q1 dependency contract into a small acceptance matrix first, so later iterations can validate execution readiness instead of re-deriving sequencing
   189	
   190	## Recommended Next Focus
   191	Convert Q1 into implementation-ready slices: define the markdown-integrity issue schema, the operator-facing command/hint surface, and the explicit handoff contract that `spec-kit doctor` will consume in Q2.
   192	
   193	RESEARCH BRIEF:
   194	# $refine TIDD-EC Prompt: 002-mex-main
   195	
   196	## 2. Role
   197	
   198	You are a research specialist in knowledge scaffold architectures, drift detection systems, markdown-based persistence, and codebase-knowledge synchronization. Work like a systems analyst who can separate scaffold design, static drift checking, and targeted AI repair loops, then translate Mex's concrete mechanisms into practical improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`.
   199	
   200	## 3. Task
   201	
   202	Research Mex's markdown scaffold and drift detection patterns to identify practical, evidence-backed improvements for `Code_Environment/Public`, especially around staleness detection, codebase-knowledge synchronization, and structured project knowledge. Determine which Mex patterns should be `adopt now`, `prototype later`, or `reject`.
   203	
   204	## 4. Context
   205	
   206	### 4.1 System Description
   207	
   208	Mex is a structured markdown scaffold with a TypeScript/Node.js CLI for persistent AI project memory. Instead of a database, it keeps project knowledge in navigable markdown files such as `AGENTS.md`, `ROUTER.md`, `context/*.md`, and `patterns/*.md`. Its defining mechanism is drift detection: eight static checkers validate scaffold claims against the real codebase without AI or token spend. Those checkers cover path validation, frontmatter edges, pattern index consistency, staleness based on git history, command existence, dependency claims, cross-file contradictions, and script coverage. Drift starts at score `100` and deducts `-10` per error, `-3` per warning, and `-1` per info. The scaffold is designed to grow after real work through pattern creation, targeted context updates, and current-state refreshes rather than one-time setup alone.
   209	
   210	### 4.2 Cross-Phase Awareness Table
   211	
   212	| Phase | System | Core Pattern | Overlap Risk | Differentiation |
   213	|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|-----------------|
   214	| 001 | Engram | MCP memory server (Go, SQLite+FTS5) | 002 (memory persistence) | Focus tool profiles, session lifecycle, topic keys |
   215	| 002 | Mex | Markdown scaffold + drift detection | 001 (memory), 003 (memory) | Focus drift detection, scaffold structure, no-DB approach |
   216	| 003 | Modus Memory | FSRS spaced repetition + BM25 | 001 (FTS5), 004 (local memory) | Focus FSRS decay, cross-referencing, librarian expansion |
   217	| 004 | OpenCode Mnemosyne | Hybrid search (FTS5 + vector) | 001 (FTS5), 003 (local memory) | Focus vector search, plugin architecture, scoping |
   218	
   219	### 4.3 What This Repo Already Has
   220

     2	import { computeScore } from "../src/drift/scoring.js";
     3	import type { DriftIssue } from "../src/types.js";
     4	
     5	function issue(severity: DriftIssue["severity"]): DriftIssue {

     7	    code: "MISSING_PATH",
     8	    severity,

    10	    line: 1,
    11	    message: "test",

    15	describe("computeScore", () => {

    17	    expect(computeScore([])).toBe(100);
    18	  });

    20	  it("deducts 10 per error", () => {

    22	    expect(computeScore([issue("error"), issue("error")])).toBe(80);

    24	
    25	  it("deducts 3 per warning", () => {

    30	    expect(computeScore([issue("info")])).toBe(99);
    31	  });

     1	import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from "vitest";
     2	import { mkdtempSync, writeFileSync, rmSync } from "node:fs";
     3	import { join } from "node:path";
     4	import { tmpdir } from "node:os";
     5	import { extractClaims } from "../src/drift/claims.js";
     6	
     7	let tmpDir: string;
     8	
     9	beforeEach(() => {
    10	  tmpDir = mkdtempSync(join(tmpdir(), "mex-test-"));
    11	});
    12	
    13	afterEach(() => {
    14	  rmSync(tmpDir, { recursive: true, force: true });
    15	});
    16	
    17	function writeFixture(name: string, content: string): string {
    18	  const path = join(tmpDir, name);
    19	  writeFileSync(path, content);
    20	  return path;
    21	}
    22	
    23	describe("extractClaims — paths", () => {
    24	  it("extracts inline code paths with slashes", () => {
    25	    const path = writeFixture(
    26	      "test.md",
    27	      "# Setup\n\nRun from `src/index.ts` to start."
    28	    );
    29	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    30	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    31	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(1);
    32	    expect(paths[0].value).toBe("src/index.ts");
    33	  });
    34	
    35	  it("extracts paths with directory + known extension", () => {
    36	    const path = writeFixture(
    37	      "test.md",
    38	      "# Files\n\nSee `src/config.json` and `lib/app.py`."
    39	    );
    40	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    41	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    42	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(2);
    43	    expect(paths.map((p) => p.value)).toContain("src/config.json");
    44	    expect(paths.map((p) => p.value)).toContain("lib/app.py");
    45	  });
    46	
    47	  it("skips template placeholders with angle brackets", () => {
    48	    const path = writeFixture(
    49	      "test.md",
    50	      "# Patterns\n\nCreate `patterns/<name>.md` for each task."
    51	    );
    52	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    53	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    54	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(0);
    55	  });
    56	
    57	  it("skips template placeholders with square brackets", () => {
    58	    const path = writeFixture(
    59	      "test.md",
    60	      "# Files\n\nSee `src/[slug].tsx` for dynamic routes."
    61	    );
    62	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    63	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    64	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(0);
    65	  });
    66	
    67	  it("skips URL routes without file extensions", () => {
    68	    const path = writeFixture(
    69	      "test.md",
    70	      "# Routes\n\nEndpoints: `/voice/incoming`, `/voice/process`, `/api/users`."
    71	    );
    72	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    73	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    74	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(0);
    75	  });
    76	
    77	  it("skips code snippets with parentheses or equals", () => {
    78	    const path = writeFixture(
    79	      "test.md",
    80	      "# Code\n\nUse `response.redirect(\"/next\")` and `base_url: str = os.getenv(\"FOO\")`."
    81	    );
    82	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    83	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    84	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(0);
    85	  });
    86	
    87	  it("skips wildcard patterns like *_client.py", () => {
    88	    const path = writeFixture(
    89	      "test.md",
    90	      "# Clients\n\nAll files matching `*_streaming_client.py`."
    91	    );
    92	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
    93	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");
    94	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(0);
    95	  });
    96	
    97	  it("extracts bare filenames as path claims", () => {
    98	    const path = writeFixture(
    99	      "test.md",
   100	      "# Files\n\nSee `pipeline.py` and `server.py` for details."
   101	    );
   102	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
   103	    const paths = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "path");

   111	      "test.md",
   112	      "# Files\n\nSee `api_clients/groq_client.py` for the implementation."
   113	    );
   114	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");

   119

   122	      "test.md",

   127	    expect(paths).toHaveLength(1);
   128	    expect(paths[0].negated).toBe(true);
   129	  });
   130	});
   131	
   132	describe("extractClaims — commands", () => {
   133	  it("extracts npm run commands from inline code", () => {
   134	    const path = writeFixture(
   135	      "test.md",
   136	      "# Setup\n\nRun `npm run build` to compile."
   137	    );
   138	    const claims = extractClaims(path, "test.md");
   139	    const cmds = claims.filter((c) => c.kind === "command");
   140	    expect(cmds).toHaveLength(1);
   141	    expect(cmds[0].value).toBe("npm run build");
   142	  });
   143	
   144	  it("extracts commands from code blocks", () => {
   145	    const path = writeFixture(
   146	      "test.md",
   147	      "# Setup\n\n```sh\nnpm install\nnpm run dev\n```"
   148	    );

     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// 1. TEST — ABLATION FRAMEWORK
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	//
     5	// Unit and integration tests for the ablation study framework.
     6	// Verifies feature flag gating, channel-to-flag mapping, ablation runner
     7	// With dependency-injected mock search functions, report formatting,
     8	// And DB persistence of ablation results.
     9	//
    10	// Acceptance criterion: ablation run shows per-channel Recall@20 delta
    11	// And can isolate the contribution of at least 1 individual channel.
    12	
    13	import { describe, it, expect, vi, beforeEach, afterEach, beforeAll, afterAll } from 'vitest';
    14	import * as path from 'path';
    15	import * as os from 'os';
    16	import * as fs from 'fs';
    17	import Database from 'better-sqlite3';
    18	
    19	import {
    20	  isAblationEnabled,
    21	  inspectGroundTruthAlignment,
    22	  assertGroundTruthAlignment,
    23	  runAblation,
    24	  storeAblationResults,
    25	  formatAblationReport,
    26	  toHybridSearchFlags,
    27	  ALL_CHANNELS,
    28	} from '../lib/eval/ablation-framework';
    29	import type {
    30	  AblationChannel,
    31	  AblationSearchFn,
    32	  AblationConfig,
    33	  AblationReport,
    34	  AblationResult,
    35	  AblationMetrics,
    36	  AblationMetricEntry,
    37	} from '../lib/eval/ablation-framework';
    38	import type { EvalResult } from '../lib/eval/eval-metrics';
    39	import { initEvalDb, closeEvalDb, getEvalDb } from '../lib/eval/eval-db';
    40	
    41	// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    42	// HELPERS
    43	// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    44	
    45	/**
    46	 * Create a mock search function that returns different results
    47	 * depending on which channels are disabled.
    48	 *
    49	 * When all channels active (baseline): returns memoryIds 1..count (high quality).
    50	 * When a channel is disabled: drops some results to simulate quality degradation.
    51	 *
    52	 * @param relevantIds - The set of memory IDs that are "relevant" in ground truth.
    53	 * @param channelContributions - Map of channel name to the IDs that channel uniquely provides.
    54	 */
    55	function createMockSearchFn(
    56	  relevantIds: number[],
    57	  channelContributions: Map<AblationChannel, number[]>,
    58	): AblationSearchFn {
    59	  return (query: string, disabledChannels: Set<AblationChannel>): EvalResult[] => {
    60	    // Start with all relevant IDs as results
    61	    let resultIds = [...relevantIds];
    62	
    63	    // When a channel is disabled, remove the IDs it contributes
    64	    for (const [channel, ids] of channelContributions) {
    65	      if (disabledChannels.has(channel)) {
    66	        const removeSet = new Set(ids);
    67	        resultIds = resultIds.filter(id => !removeSet.has(id));
    68	      }
    69	    }
    70	
    71	    // Return as EvalResult[] with descending scores
    72	    return resultIds.map((memoryId, idx) => ({
    73	      memoryId,
    74	      score: 1.0 - idx * 0.01,
    75	      rank: idx + 1,
    76	    }));
    77	  };
    78	}
    79	
    80	/**
    81	 * Build mock AblationMetrics for testing multi-metric report/storage.
    82	 */
    83	function buildMockMetrics(recallDelta: number): AblationMetrics {
    84	  function entry(b: number, delta: number): AblationMetricEntry {
    85	    return { baseline: b, ablated: b + delta, delta };
    86	  }
    87	  return {
    88	    'MRR@5': entry(0.65, recallDelta * 0.8),
    89	    'precision@5': entry(0.5, recallDelta * 0.7),
    90	    'recall@5': entry(0.6, recallDelta),
    91	    'NDCG@5': entry(0.7, recallDelta * 0.9),
    92	    'MAP': entry(0.55, recallDelta * 0.6),
    93	    'hit_rate': entry(0.9, recallDelta * 0.3),
    94	    'latency_p50': entry(12, 0.5),
    95	    'latency_p95': entry(25, 1.2),
    96	    'token_usage': entry(0, 0),
    97	  };
    98	}
    99	
   100	/**
   101	 * Build a minimal AblationReport for testing formatAblationReport and storeAblationResults.
   102	 */
   103	function buildMockReport(overrides: Partial<AblationReport> = {}): AblationReport {
   104	  const defaultResults: AblationResult[] = [
   105	    {
   106	      channel: 'vector',
   107	      baselineRecall20: 0.8,
   108	      ablatedRecall20: 0.5,
   109	      delta: -0.3,
   110	      pValue: 0.01,
   111	      queriesChannelHelped: 8,
   112	      queriesChannelHurt: 1,
   113	      queriesUnchanged: 1,
   114	      queryCount: 10,
   115	      metrics: buildMockMetrics(-0.3),
   116	    },
   117	    {
   118	      channel: 'bm25',
   119	      baselineRecall20: 0.8,
   120	      ablatedRecall20: 0.75,
   121	      delta: -0.05,
   122	      pValue: 0.12,
   123	      queriesChannelHelped: 4,
   124	      queriesChannelHurt: 2,
   125	      queriesUnchanged: 4,
   126	      queryCount: 10,
   127	      metrics: buildMockMetrics(-0.05),
   128	    },
   129	    {
   130	      channel: 'fts5',
   131	      baselineRecall20: 0.8,
   132	      ablatedRecall20: 0.82,
   133	      delta: 0.02,
   134	      pValue: 0.45,
   135	      queriesChannelHelped: 2,
   136	      queriesChannelHurt: 3,
   137	      queriesUnchanged: 5,
   138	      queryCount: 10,
   139	      metrics: buildMockMetrics(0.02),
   140	    },
   141	  ];
   142	
   143	  return {
   144	    timestamp: '2026-02-28T12:00:00.000Z',
   145	    runId: 'ablation-test-1234',
   146	    config: { channels: ['vector', 'bm25', 'fts5'] as AblationChannel[] },
   147	    results: defaultResults,
   148	    overallBaselineRecall: 0.8,
   149	    queryCount: 10,
   150	    durationMs: 500,
   151	    ...overrides,
   152	  };
   153	}
   154	
   155	/** Saved env state for clean restoration. */
   156	let savedAblationEnv: string | undefined;
   157	
   158	// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   159	// UNIT TESTS
   160	// ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   161	
   162	describe('Ablation Framework (R13-S3)', () => {
   163

   172	    } else {
   173	      delete process.env.SPECKIT_ABLATION;
   174	    }
   175	  });
   176	
   177	  // 1. isAblationEnabled() — feature flag gating
   178	  describe('isAblationEnabled()', () => {
   179	    it('returns false when SPECKIT_ABLATION is not set', () => {
   180	      delete process.env.SPECKIT_ABLATION;
   181	      expect(isAblationEnabled()).toBe(false);
   182	    });
   183	
   184	    it('returns true when SPECKIT_ABLATION=true', () => {
   185	      process.env.SPECKIT_ABLATION = 'true';
   186	      expect(isAblationEnabled()).toBe(true);
   187	    });
   188	
   189	    it('returns true when SPECKIT_ABLATION=TRUE (case-insensitive)', () => {
   190	      process.env.SPECKIT_ABLATION = 'TRUE';
   191	      expect(isAblationEnabled()).toBe(true);
   192	    });
   193	
   194	    it('returns true when SPECKIT_ABLATION=True (mixed case)', () => {
   195	      process.env.SPECKIT_ABLATION = 'True';
   196	      expect(isAblationEnabled()).toBe(true);

   198	
   199	    it('returns false when SPECKIT_ABLATION=false', () => {

   201	      expect(isAblationEnabled()).toBe(false);
   202	    });
   203	
   204	    it('returns false when SPECKIT_ABLATION=1', () => {
   205	      process.env.SPECKIT_ABLATION = '1';
   206	      expect(isAblationEnabled()).toBe(false);

   208	
   209	    it('returns false when SPECKIT_ABLATION is empty string', () => {

   213	  });
   214	
   215	  // 2. ALL_CHANNELS constant
   216	  describe('ALL_CHANNELS', () => {

     2	// Ensures main() preserves explicit CLI targets into runWorkflow

     7	const harness = vi.hoisted(() => ({

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// TEST: INTEGRATION SEARCH PIPELINE
     2	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
     3	
     4	import * as searchHandler from '../handlers/memory-search';
     5	import * as hybridSearch from '../lib/search/hybrid-search';
     6	import * as vectorIndex from '../lib/search/vector-index';
     7	import * as bm25Index from '../lib/search/bm25-index';
     8	import type { MCPResponse } from '../handlers/types';
     9	import { isMMREnabled, isTRMEnabled, isMultiQueryEnabled } from '../lib/search/search-flags';
    10	
    11	interface SearchEnvelopePayload {
    12	  error?: unknown;
    13	  results?: unknown;
    14	}
    15	
    16	async function withTimeout<T>(promise: Promise<T>, ms: number, name: string): Promise<T> {
    17	  return Promise.race([
    18	    promise,
    19	    new Promise<T>((_, reject) =>
    20	      setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(`Timeout: ${name} exceeded ${ms}ms`)), ms)
    21	    ),
    22	  ]);
    23	}
    24	
    25	function getErrorMessage(error: unknown): string {
    26	  return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    27	}
    28	
    29	function getErrorCode(error: unknown): string | undefined {
    30	  if (typeof error !== 'object' || error === null || !('code' in error)) {
    31	    return undefined;
    32	  }
    33	  const code = error.code;
    34	  return typeof code === 'string' ? code : undefined;
    35	}
    36	
    37	function parseResponsePayload(response: MCPResponse): SearchEnvelopePayload {
    38	  return JSON.parse(response.content[0].text) as SearchEnvelopePayload;
    39	}

    70	        // DB/infra errors are acceptable — input validation errors are not

    73	          message.includes('database') ||
    74	          message.includes('SQLITE') ||
    75	          message.includes('DB') ||
    76	          message.includes('no such table') ||
    77	          message.includes('embed') ||
    78	          message.includes('initialize') ||
    79	          getErrorCode(error) === 'E010' ||
    80	          getErrorCode(error) === 'E020';
    81	        const isTimeout = message.includes('Timeout');
    82	
    83	        if (!isInfraError && !isTimeout) {
    84	          expect.unreachable(`Unexpected error: ${message}`);
    85	        }
    86	      }
    87	    });

   125	
   126	  it('removes deleted tracked files during incremental scans', async () => {
   127	    mocks.execSyncMock.mockReturnValue('same-head\n');

   129	    mocks.getTrackedFilesMock.mockReturnValue(['/workspace/current.ts', '/workspace/deleted.ts']);
   130	    mocks.existsSyncMock.mockImplementation((filePath: string) => filePath !== '/workspace/deleted.ts');

   133	      rootDir: process.cwd(),
   134	      incremental: true,
   135	    });

   137	    const payload = JSON.parse(response.content[0].text) as {
   138	      status: string;

   141	        filesSkipped: number;
   142	        fullReindexTriggered: boolean;
   143	      };
   144	    };
   145

   147	    expect(payload.data.filesIndexed).toBe(0);

   151	    expect(mocks.upsertFileMock).not.toHaveBeenCalled();
   152	  });

     1	import { afterAll, afterEach, beforeAll, beforeEach, describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
     2	import * as handler from '../handlers/memory-crud';

     6	
     7	/** Parse the JSON response text from an MCP response. */
     8	function parseResponse(result: { content: Array<{ text: string }> }) {
     9	  return JSON.parse(result.content[0].text);
    10	}
    11	
    12	function getErrorMessage(parsed: Record<string, unknown>) {
    13	  const data = parsed.data as { error?: unknown } | undefined;
    14	  return (typeof data?.error === 'string' ? data.error : parsed.error) as string | undefined;
    15	}
    16	
    17	function getDetails(parsed: Record<string, unknown>) {
    18	  const data = parsed.data as { details?: Record<string, unknown> } | undefined;
    19	  return (data?.details ?? parsed.details) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
    20	}
    21	
    22	beforeAll(() => {
    23	  vectorIndex.closeDb();
    24	  vectorIndex.initializeDb(':memory:');
    25	});
    26	
    27	afterAll(() => {
    28	  vectorIndex.closeDb();
    29	});

    33	});
    34

    36	  vi.restoreAllMocks();
    37	});

    39	describe('handleMemoryHealth Edge Cases (T007b)', () => {
    40	  it('T007b-H1: Invalid reportMode returns error response with requestId', async () => {
    41	    const result = await handler.handleMemoryHealth({ reportMode: 'not-valid' } as unknown as HealthArgs);
    42	    const parsed = parseResponse(result);
    43	    const error = getErrorMessage(parsed);
    44	    const details = getDetails(parsed);
    45	    expect(error).toMatch(/Invalid reportMode/);
    46	    expect(details?.requestId).toBeDefined();
    47	    expect(typeof details?.requestId).toBe('string');
    48	  });
    49	
    50	  it('T007b-H2: Zero limit returns error response with requestId', async () => {
    51	    const result = await handler.handleMemoryHealth({ reportMode: 'divergent_aliases', limit: 0 });
    52	    const parsed = parseResponse(result);
    53	    const error = getErrorMessage(parsed);
    54	    const details = getDetails(parsed);

    56	    expect(details?.requestId).toBeDefined();
    57	  });
    58	
    59	  it('T007b-H3: Negative limit returns error response', async () => {
    60	    const result = await handler.handleMemoryHealth({ limit: -5 } as HealthArgs);

    76	  });
    77	
    78	  it('T007b-H5: Non-boolean confirmed returns error', async () => {

    87	
    88	  it('T007b-H6: Non-string specFolder returns error with requestId', async () => {

    95	  });
    96	
    97	  it('T007b-H7: divergent_aliases reportMode returns compact success payload', async () => {
    98	    const result = await handler.handleMemoryHealth({ reportMode: 'divergent_aliases' });
    99	    const parsed = parseResponse(result);
   100	    expect(result.isError).toBe(false);
   101	    expect(parsed.data.reportMode).toBe('divergent_aliases');
   102	    expect(parsed.data.limit).toBe(20);
   103	    expect(Array.isArray(parsed.data.groups)).toBe(true);

   105	
   106	  it('T007b-H8: Empty args return the default full health payload', async () => {

   136	    vi.spyOn(core, 'checkDatabaseUpdated').mockRejectedValue(new Error('marker read failed'));

    60	async function withTimeout<T>(promise: Promise<T>, ms: number, name: string): Promise<T> {
    61	  return Promise.race([
    62	    promise,
    63	    new Promise<T>((_, reject) =>
    64	      setTimeout(() => reject(new Error(`Timeout: ${name} exceeded ${ms}ms`)), ms)
    65	    ),
    66	  ]);
    67	}
    68	
    69	type ParsedContextResponse = {
    70	  meta: {
    71	    mode?: string;
    72	    requestedMode?: string;
    73	    tokenUsageSource?: string;
    74	    tokenUsagePressure?: number | null;
    75	    pressureLevel?: string;
    76	    pressurePolicy: {
    77	      applied?: boolean;
    78	      overrideMode?: string;
    79	      warning?: string | null;
    80	    };
    81	    sessionLifecycle: {
    82	      sessionScope?: string;
    83	      requestedSessionId?: string | null;
    84	      effectiveSessionId?: string;
    85	      resumed?: boolean;
    86	      eventCounterStart?: number;
    87	      resumedContextCount?: number;
    88	    };
    89	  };
    90	  data: {
    91	    mode?: string;
    92	    strategy?: string;
    93	    content?: Array<{ text?: string }>;
    94	    systemPromptContextInjected?: boolean;
    95	    systemPromptContext?: Array<{ memoryId: number }>;
    96	    details?: {
    97	      layer?: string;
    98	    };
    99	  };
   100	  hints?: string[];
   101	  error?: boolean;
   102	  mode?: string;
   103	};
   104	
   105	function parseResponse(result: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof handler.handleMemoryContext>>): ParsedContextResponse {
   106	  return JSON.parse(result.content[0].text) as ParsedContextResponse;
   107	}

   135	    expect(bm25Payload!.hitCount).toBe(2);
   136	  });
   137	
   138	  it('buildEvalChannelPayloads falls back to hybrid channel when attribution is missing', () => {

   141	      { id: 2, similarity: 0.4 },
   142	    ]);

   145	    const [payload] = payloads;
   146	    expect(payload).toBeDefined();
   147	    expect(payload!.channel).toBe('hybrid');

   149	  });

     1	import fs from 'fs';
     2	import os from 'os';
     3	import path from 'path';

     5	
     6	import * as handler from '../handlers/memory-save';
     7	import * as vectorIndex from '../lib/search/vector-index';

     9	import {
    10	  evaluateMemorySufficiency,
    11	  type MemoryEvidenceSnapshot,
    12	} from '@spec-kit/shared/parsing/memory-sufficiency';
    13	import { validateMemoryTemplateContract } from '@spec-kit/shared/parsing/memory-template-contract';
    14	import {
    15	  computeMemoryQualityScore,
    16	  runQualityLoop,
    17	  scoreCoherence,
    18	  scoreTriggerPhrases,
    19	} from '../handlers/quality-loop';
    20	import {
    21	  runQualityGate,
    22	  validateStructural,
    23	  scoreContentQuality,
    24	  checkSemanticDedup,
    25	  resetActivationTimestamp,
    26	  setActivationTimestamp,
    27	} from '../lib/validation/save-quality-gate';
    28	
    29	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30	// TEST INFRASTRUCTURE
    31	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32	
    33	const TEST_DB_DIR = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `speckit-pipeline-enforcement-${process.pid}`);
    34	const TEST_DB_PATH = path.join(TEST_DB_DIR, 'speckit-memory.db');
    35	const FIXTURE_ROOT = path.join(process.cwd(), 'tmp-test-fixtures', 'specs', '998-pipeline-enforcement');
    36	
    37	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38	// FIXTURE BUILDER
    39	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	
    41	interface PipelineMemoryOverrides {
    42	  title?: string;
    43	  description?: string;
    44	  triggerPhrases?: string[];
    45	  importanceTier?: string;
    46	  contextType?: string;
    47	  removeSections?: string[];
    48	  removeAnchors?: string[];
    49	  replaceContent?: string;
    50	  removeFrontmatter?: boolean;
    51	  removeFrontmatterKey?: string;
    52	  triggerPhrasesFormat?: 'list' | 'string' | 'empty_array';
    53	  insertMustache?: boolean;
    54	  missingBlankLineAfterFrontmatter?: boolean;
    55	  fileTableEntries?: Array<{ path: string; description: string }>;
    56	  observations?: Array<{ title: string; narrative: string }>;
    57	  decisions?: string[];
    58	  contentSuffix?: string;
    59	}
    60	
    61	function buildValidPipelineMemory(overrides: PipelineMemoryOverrides = {}): string {
    62	  const {
    63	    title = 'Memory Save Pipeline Enforcement Test Fixture',
    64	    description = 'Durable enforcement fixture that validates the full save pipeline rejects malformed content at every gate.',
    65	    triggerPhrases = ['pipeline-enforcement', 'save-pipeline-test', 'memory-gate-validation', 'enforcement-fixture'],
    66	    importanceTier = 'normal',
    67	    contextType = 'implementation',
    68	    removeSections = [],
    69	    removeAnchors = [],
    70	    removeFrontmatter = false,
    71	    removeFrontmatterKey,
    72	    triggerPhrasesFormat = 'list',
    73	    insertMustache = false,
    74	    missingBlankLineAfterFrontmatter = false,
    75	    fileTableEntries = [
    76	      { path: 'mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts', description: 'Coordinates atomic save, sufficiency enforcement, duplicate detection, and post-mutation feedback for the memory save path.' },
    77	      { path: 'mcp_server/lib/validation/save-quality-gate.ts', description: 'Three-layer quality gate that validates structure, content density, and semantic deduplication before persisting.' },
    78	    ],
    79	    observations = [
    80	      {
    81	        title: 'Observation: pipeline gate ordering',
    82	        narrative: 'The save pipeline enforces gates in strict sequence: parser validation, quality loop scoring, sufficiency evaluation, template contract validation, and save quality gate. Each gate blocks downstream processing on failure.',
    83	      },
    84	    ],
    85	    decisions = [
    86	      'Decided to enforce template contract validation after sufficiency checks so that structurally valid but semantically empty memories are caught early without incurring template validation overhead.',
    87	    ],
    88	    contentSuffix = '',
    89	  } = overrides;
    90	
    91	  // Build frontmatter
    92	  let frontmatter = '';
    93	  if (!removeFrontmatter) {
    94	    const triggerPhrasesYaml = triggerPhrasesFormat === 'string'
    95	      ? `trigger_phrases: "${triggerPhrases.join(', ')}"`
    96	      : triggerPhrasesFormat === 'empty_array'
    97	        ? 'trigger_phrases: []'
    98	        : `trigger_phrases:\n${triggerPhrases.map(tp => `  - "${tp}"`).join('\n')}`;
    99	
   100	    const parts: string[] = [];
   101	    if (removeFrontmatterKey !== 'title') {
   102	      parts.push(`title: "${title}"`);
   103	    }
   104	    if (removeFrontmatterKey !== 'description') {
   105	      parts.push(`description: "${description}"`);
   106	    }
   107	    parts.push(triggerPhrasesYaml);
   108	    if (removeFrontmatterKey !== 'importance_tier') {
   109	      parts.push(`importance_tier: "${importanceTier}"`);
   110	    }
   111	    if (removeFrontmatterKey !== 'contextType') {
   112	      parts.push(`contextType: "${contextType}"`);
   113	    }
   114	
   115	    frontmatter = `---\n${parts.join('\n')}\n---`;
   116	  }
   117	
   118	  // When missingBlankLineAfterFrontmatter=true, the frontmatter close (---) is immediately
   119	  // followed by body content (after the regex-consumed newline). When false, an extra newline
   120	  // is inserted creating a blank line between frontmatter and body.
   121	  const separator = missingBlankLineAfterFrontmatter ? '' : '\n';
   122	
   123	  // Build mandatory sections
   124	  const mandatorySections: Record<string, string> = {};
   125	
   126	  // SESSION SUMMARY
   127	  mandatorySections['session-summary'] = `
   128	| **Meta Data** | **Value** |
   129	|:--------------|:----------|
   130	| Total Messages | 12 |
   131	| Fixture Type | pipeline-enforcement |
   132	`;
   133	
   134	  // CONTINUE SESSION
   135	  mandatorySections['continue-session'] = `
   136	${removeAnchors.includes('continue-session') ? '' : '<!-- ANCHOR:continue-session -->'}
   137	
   138	## CONTINUE SESSION
   139	
   140	Continue validating the memory save pipeline enforcement test suite to ensure all six gates properly reject malformed content without allowing bypass.
   141	
   142	${removeAnchors.includes('continue-session') ? '' : '<!-- /ANCHOR:continue-session -->'}
   143	`;
   144	
   145	  // CANONICAL SOURCES (new compact format)
   146	  mandatorySections['canonical-docs'] = `
   147	${removeAnchors.includes('canonical-docs') ? '' : '<!-- ANCHOR:canonical-docs -->'}
   148	
   149	## CANONICAL SOURCES
   150	
   151	- \`decision-record.md\` — Pipeline architecture and enforcement strategy decisions
   152	- \`implementation-summary.md\` — Build story and gate integration
   153	
   154	${removeAnchors.includes('canonical-docs') ? '' : '<!-- /ANCHOR:canonical-docs -->'}
   155	`;
   156	
   157	  mandatorySections['key-files'] = `
   158	## KEY FILES
   159	
   160	| File | Description |
   161	|:-----|:------------|
   162	${fileTableEntries.map((entry) => `| \`${entry.path}\` | ${entry.description} |`).join('\n')}
   163	`;
   164	
   165	  // OVERVIEW
   166	  mandatorySections['overview'] = `
   167	${removeAnchors.includes('overview') ? '' : '<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->'}
   168	
   169	## OVERVIEW
   170	
   171	This pipeline enforcement fixture validates that every gate in the memory save pipeline correctly rejects malformed content. The gates include parser validation, quality loop scoring, sufficiency evaluation, template contract validation, and the save quality gate.
   172	
   173	${removeAnchors.includes('overview') ? '' : '<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->'}
   174	`;
   175	
   176	  // DISTINGUISHING EVIDENCE (new compact format)
   177	  mandatorySections['evidence'] = `
   178	${removeAnchors.includes('evidence') ? '' : '<!-- ANCHOR:evidence -->'}
   179	
   180	## DISTINGUISHING EVIDENCE
   181	
   182	- Validated all six enforcement gates in the save pipeline
   183	- Confirmed atomic save rollback on rejection
   184	- Tested malformed frontmatter, insufficient content, and template violations
   185	
   186	${removeAnchors.includes('evidence') ? '' : '<!-- /ANCHOR:evidence -->'}
   187	`;
   188	
   189	  mandatorySections['decisions'] = `
   190	## DECISIONS
   191	
   192	${decisions.map((decision) => `- ${decision}`).join('\n')}
   193	`;
   194	
   195	  mandatorySections['outcomes'] = `
   196	## KEY OUTCOMES
   197	
   198	- Verified parser validation runs before all downstream gates.
   199	- Verified sufficiency rejects thin or generic content before template and semantic validation.
   200	- Verified template contract failures block the save-quality gate when evidence remains sufficient.
   201	`;
   202	
   203	  // DETAILED CHANGES, DECISIONS, CONVERSATION are no longer mandatory in compact format
   204	  // They can be omitted or included as optional conditional sections
   205	  // For testing purposes, we'll omit them to validate the compact contract
   206	
   207	  // RECOVERY HINTS
   208	  mandatorySections['recovery-hints'] = `
   209	${removeAnchors.includes('recovery-hints') ? '' : '<!-- ANCHOR:recovery-hints -->'}
   210	
   211	## RECOVERY HINTS
   212	
   213	- If this fixture starts failing insufficiency again, add more concrete file, decision, blocker, next action, or outcome evidence instead of weakening the gate.
   214	- Re-run the pipeline enforcement suite and the full MCP package test suite after changing the save contract.
   215	
   216	${removeAnchors.includes('recovery-hints') ? '' : '<!-- /ANCHOR:recovery-hints -->'}
   217	`;
   218	
   219	  // MEMORY METADATA
   220	  mandatorySections['memory-metadata'] = `
   221	${removeAnchors.includes('metadata') ? '' : '<!-- ANCHOR:metadata -->'}
   222	
   223	## MEMORY METADATA
   224	
   225	\`\`\`yaml
   226	session_id: "pipeline-enforcement-fixture"
   227	fixture_title: "${title}"
   228	\`\`\`
   229	
   230	${removeAnchors.includes('metadata') ? '' : '<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->'}
   231	`;
   232	
   233	  // Remove sections that should be stripped
   234	  for (const sectionKey of removeSections) {
   235	    delete mandatorySections[sectionKey];
   236	  }
   237	
   238	  if (overrides.replaceContent !== undefined) {
   239	    return overrides.replaceContent;
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  // Assemble the full content
   243	  const body = `
   244	# ${title}
   245	
   246	## SESSION SUMMARY
   247	${mandatorySections['session-summary'] ?? ''}
   248	${mandatorySections['continue-session'] ?? ''}
   249	${mandatorySections['canonical-docs'] ?? ''}
   250	${mandatorySections['key-files'] ?? ''}
   251	${mandatorySections['overview'] ?? ''}
   252	${mandatorySections['evidence'] ?? ''}
   253	${mandatorySections['decisions'] ?? ''}
   254	${mandatorySections['outcomes'] ?? ''}
   255	${mandatorySections['recovery-hints'] ?? ''}
   256	${mandatorySections['memory-metadata'] ?? ''}
   257	${contentSuffix}`;
   258	
   259	  let content = removeFrontmatter ? body : `${frontmatter}${separator}${body}`;
   260

     2	import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
     3	
     4	import type { MCPResponse } from '../handlers/types';

    11	interface TriggerEnvelopeData {
    12	  error?: string;
    13	}
    14	
    15	function getErrorMessage(error: unknown): string {
    16	  return error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
    17	}
    18	
    19	function parseEnvelope(response: MCPResponse): MCPEnvelope<TriggerEnvelopeData> {
    20	  return JSON.parse(response.content[0].text) as MCPEnvelope<TriggerEnvelopeData>;

    53	
    54	describe('Memory Search Integration (T601-T650) [deferred - requires DB test fixtures]', () => {
    55	
    56	  describe('T601-T610 - Testing Effect Formula', () => {
    57	    it('T601: GRADE_GOOD constant is 3', () => {
    58	      expect(fsrsScheduler.GRADE_GOOD).toBe(3);
    59	    });
    60	
    61	    it('T602: Difficulty bonus calculation correct', () => {
    62	      const testCases = [
    63	        { r: 0.0, expected: 0.45 },
    64	        { r: 0.2, expected: 0.35 },
    65	        { r: 0.5, expected: 0.20 },
    66	        { r: 0.9, expected: 0.00 },
    67	        { r: 1.0, expected: 0.00 },
    68	      ];
    69	      for (const tc of testCases) {
    70	        const calculated = Math.max(0, (0.9 - tc.r) * 0.5);
    71	        expect(Math.abs(calculated - tc.expected)).toBeLessThan(0.001);
    72	      }
    73	    });
    74	
    75	    it('T603: GRADE_GOOD increases stability', () => {
    76	      const initial = fsrsScheduler.createInitialParams();
    77	      const reviewed = fsrsScheduler.processReview(initial, fsrsScheduler.GRADE_GOOD);
    78	      expect(reviewed.stability).toBeGreaterThan(initial.stability);
    79	    });
    80	
    81	    it('T604: Stability multiplier applied with difficulty bonus', () => {
    82	      const lowRetrievability = fsrsScheduler.updateStability(2.0, 5.0, fsrsScheduler.GRADE_GOOD, 0.2);
    83	      const highRetrievability = fsrsScheduler.updateStability(2.0, 5.0, fsrsScheduler.GRADE_GOOD, 0.9);
    84	      expect(lowRetrievability).toBeGreaterThan(highRetrievability);
    85	    });
    86	
    87	    it('T605: Formula handles edge cases', () => {
    88	      expect(fsrsScheduler.calculateRetrievability(-1, 5)).toBe(0);
    89	      expect(fsrsScheduler.calculateOptimalInterval(0, 0.9)).toBe(0);
    90	    });
    91	  });
    92	
    93	  describe('T611-T620 - Desirable Difficulty', () => {
    94	    it('T611: Low R (0.2) gives ~0.35 bonus', () => {
    95	      const bonus = Math.max(0, (0.9 - 0.2) * 0.5);
    96	      expect(Math.abs(bonus - 0.35)).toBeLessThan(0.001);
    97	    });
    98	
    99	    it('T612: High R (0.9) gives ~0.0 bonus', () => {
   100	      const bonus = Math.max(0, (0.9 - 0.9) * 0.5);
   101	      expect(Math.abs(bonus - 0.0)).toBeLessThan(0.001);
   102	    });
   103	
   104	    it('T613: R = 1.0 gives zero difficulty bonus', () => {
   105	      const bonus = Math.max(0, (0.9 - 1.0) * 0.5);
   106	      expect(bonus).toBe(0);
   107	    });
   108	
   109	    it('T614: R = 0 gives maximum bonus (0.45)', () => {
   110	      const bonus = Math.max(0, (0.9 - 0.0) * 0.5);
   111	      expect(Math.abs(bonus - 0.45)).toBeLessThan(0.001);
   112	    });
   113	
   114	    it('T615: Bonus capped at reasonable level', () => {
   115	      const bonusExtreme = Math.max(0, (0.9 - (-1.0)) * 0.5);
   116	      expect(bonusExtreme).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1.0);
   117	    });
   118	
   119	    it('T616: Bonus decreases monotonically with R', () => {
   120	      const rValues = [0.0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8, 0.9, 1.0];
   121	      const bonuses = rValues.map(r => Math.max(0, (0.9 - r) * 0.5));
   122	      for (let i = 1; i < bonuses.length; i++) {
   123	        expect(bonuses[i]).toBeLessThanOrEqual(bonuses[i - 1]);
   124	      }
   125	    });
   126	
   127	    it('T617: Difficulty bonus formula matches spec', () => {
   128	      const actual = Math.max(0, (0.9 - 0.5) * 0.5);
   129	      expect(Math.abs(actual - 0.2)).toBeLessThan(0.001);
   130	    });
   131	
   132	    it('T618: Combined boost = base_stability * (1 + difficultyBonus)', () => {
   133	      const baseStability = 2.0;
   134	      const difficultyBonus = Math.max(0, (0.9 - 0.2) * 0.5);
   135	      const combined = baseStability * (1 + difficultyBonus);
   136	      expect(Math.abs(combined - 2.7)).toBeLessThan(0.01);
   137	    });
   138	
   139	    it('T619: FSRS stability bounds (0.1 to 365) respected', () => {
   140	      const reviewed = fsrsScheduler.processReview({
   141	        stability: 0.01,
   142	        difficulty: 5,
   143	        lastReview: null,
   144	        reviewCount: 0,
   145	      }, fsrsScheduler.GRADE_AGAIN);
   146	      expect(reviewed.stability).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(fsrsScheduler.MIN_STABILITY);
   147	      expect(fsrsScheduler.calculateRetrievability(365, 30)).toBeLessThanOrEqual(1);
   148	    });
   149	
   150	    it('T620: Negative R handled in bonus calculation', () => {
   151	      const bonus = Math.max(0, (0.9 - (-0.5)) * 0.5);
   152	      expect(bonus).toBeGreaterThan(0);
   153	    });
   154	  });

    76	type TestDatabase = InstanceType<typeof Database>;
    77	
    78	/** Create a fresh test database with memory_index table + FTS5. */
    79	function createTestDb(): { db: TestDatabase; path: string } {
    80	  const tmpPath = path.join(os.tmpdir(), `learned-feedback-test-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}.sqlite`);
    81	  const testDb = new Database(tmpPath);
    82	
    83	  testDb.exec(`
    84	    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_index (
    85	      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    86	      title TEXT,
    87	      trigger_phrases TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
    88	      file_path TEXT DEFAULT '',
    89	      content_text TEXT DEFAULT '',
    90	      confidence REAL DEFAULT 0.5,
    91	      validation_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    92	      importance_tier TEXT DEFAULT 'normal',
    93	      importance_weight REAL DEFAULT 0.5,
    94	      spec_folder TEXT DEFAULT '',
    95	      created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    96	      updated_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    97	      is_archived INTEGER DEFAULT 0
    98	    )
    99	  `);
   100	
   101	  // Create the FTS5 table matching the real schema (NO learned_triggers!)
   102	  testDb.exec(`
   103	    CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts USING fts5(
   104	      title,
   105	      trigger_phrases,
   106	      file_path,
   107	      content_text,
   108	      content='memory_index',
   109	      content_rowid='id'
   110	    )
   111	  `);
   112	
   113	  return { db: testDb, path: tmpPath };
   114	}
   115	
   116	/** Insert a test memory with known created_at timestamp. */
   117	function insertMemory(testDb: TestDatabase, id: number, opts: {
   118	  title?: string;
   119	  triggerPhrases?: string[];
   120	  createdAt?: string;
   121	  tier?: string;
   122	  validationCount?: number;
   123	  confidence?: number;
   124	} = {}): void {
   125	  const {
   126	    title = `Memory ${id}`,
   127	    triggerPhrases = [],
   128	    createdAt = new Date(Date.now() - 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000).toISOString(), // 7 days ago default
   129	    tier = 'normal',
   130	    validationCount = 0,
   131	    confidence = 0.5,
   132	  } = opts;
   133	
   134	  testDb.prepare(`
   135	    INSERT INTO memory_index (id, title, trigger_phrases, created_at, importance_tier, validation_count, confidence)
   136	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   137	  `).run(id, title, JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases), createdAt, tier, validationCount, confidence);
   138	}

    37	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    38	// HELPERS
    39	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    40	
    41	/** Create an in-memory SQLite database with required tables. */
    42	function createTestDb(): Database.Database {
    43	  const db = new Database(':memory:');
    44	  db.exec(`
    45	    CREATE TABLE memory_index (
    46	      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    47	      spec_folder TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'test',
    48	      file_path TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'test.md',
    49	      title TEXT,
    50	      content_text TEXT,
    51	      quality_score REAL DEFAULT 0
    52	    );
    53	
    54	    CREATE TABLE causal_edges (
    55	      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    56	      source_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    57	      target_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    58	      relation TEXT NOT NULL,
    59	      strength REAL DEFAULT 1.0,
    60	      evidence TEXT,
    61	      created_by TEXT DEFAULT 'manual',
    62	      UNIQUE(source_id, target_id, relation)
    63	    );
    64	
    65	    CREATE TABLE memory_entities (
    66	      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    67	      memory_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    68	      entity_text TEXT NOT NULL,
    69	      entity_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'noun_phrase',
    70	      frequency INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    71	      created_by TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'auto',
    72	      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    73	      UNIQUE(memory_id, entity_text)
    74	    );
    75	
    76	    CREATE TABLE entity_catalog (
    77	      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    78	      canonical_name TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    79	      aliases TEXT DEFAULT '[]',
    80	      entity_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'noun_phrase',
    81	      memory_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    82	      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    83	    );
    84	  `);
    85	  return db;
    86	}
    87	
    88	/** Insert a test memory row. */
    89	function insertMemory(db: Database.Database, id: number, specFolder = 'specs/001-test', content = 'hello world'): void {
    90	  db.prepare(`
    91	    INSERT INTO memory_index (id, spec_folder, file_path, content_text)
    92	    VALUES (?, ?, 'test.md', ?)
    93	  `).run(id, specFolder, content);
    94	}
    95	
    96	/** Insert a causal edge between two nodes. */
    97	function insertEdge(db: Database.Database, sourceId: string, targetId: string, relation = 'supports'): void {
    98	  db.prepare(`
    99	    INSERT OR IGNORE INTO causal_edges (source_id, target_id, relation, strength, created_by)
   100	    VALUES (?, ?, ?, 1.0, 'test')
   101	  `).run(sourceId, targetId, relation);
   102	}
   103	
   104	/** Set SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE for the duration of a test. */
   105	function withRefreshMode(mode: string, fn: () => void): void {
   106	  const original = process.env.SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE;
   107	  process.env.SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE = mode;
   108	  try {
   109	    fn();
   110	  } finally {
   111	    if (original === undefined) {
   112	      delete process.env.SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE;
   113	    } else {
   114	      process.env.SPECKIT_GRAPH_REFRESH_MODE = original;
   115	    }
   116	  }
   117	}
   118

   208	  });

   222	  });

    43	function buildMemoryContent(title: string, body: string): string {
    44	  const titleSlug = title.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/^-|-$/g, '');
    45	  return `---
    46	title: "${title}"
    47	description: "Durable regression fixture for memory_save UX contract coverage."
    48	trigger_phrases:
    49	  - "${titleSlug}"
    50	  - "memory-save-ux"
    51	importance_tier: "normal"
    52	contextType: "implementation"
    53	---
    54	
    55	# ${title}
    56	
    57	## SESSION SUMMARY
    58	
    59	| **Meta Data** | **Value** |
    60	|:--------------|:----------|
    61	| Total Messages | 4 |
    62	| Fixture Type | memory-save-ux |
    63	
    64	<!-- ANCHOR:continue-session -->
    65	
    66	## CONTINUE SESSION
    67	
    68	Continue validating the \`memory_save\` UX contract with a fixture that is rich enough to satisfy the durable-memory gate while still exercising duplicate no-op, deferred embedding, and post-mutation feedback behavior.
    69	
    70	<!-- /ANCHOR:continue-session -->
    71	
    72	<!-- ANCHOR:canonical-docs -->
    73	
    74	## CANONICAL SOURCES
    75	
    76	- \`decision-record.md\` — UX contract and duplicate detection strategy
    77	- \`implementation-summary.md\` — Save pipeline integration story
    78	
    79	<!-- /ANCHOR:canonical-docs -->
    80	
    81	## KEY FILES
    82	
    83	| File | Description |
    84	|:-----|:------------|
    85	| \`mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts\` | Coordinates duplicate detection, sufficiency evaluation, template validation, and post-mutation feedback for \`memory_save\`. |
    86	| \`mcp_server/handlers/save/response-builder.ts\` | Shapes successful save payloads, duplicate no-op hints, and deferred embedding response details. |
    87	
    88	<!-- ANCHOR:overview -->
    89	
    90	## OVERVIEW
    91	
    92	${body}
    93	
    94	This regression fixture exists to prove that successful saves and duplicate no-op saves still report the correct UX payloads after the shared insufficiency gate and rendered-memory template contract were added to the save pipeline.
    95	
    96	<!-- /ANCHOR:overview -->
    97	
    98	<!-- ANCHOR:evidence -->
    99	
   100	## DISTINGUISHING EVIDENCE
   101	
   102	- Validated duplicate no-op response shape
   103	- Confirmed post-mutation feedback integration
   104	- Tested deferred embedding with immediate save return
   105	
   106	<!-- /ANCHOR:evidence -->
   107	
   108	## DECISIONS
   109	
   110	- Decided to keep duplicate no-op saves visible in the UX contract so callers can distinguish unchanged content from validation failures.
   111	- Decided to report deferred embedding as a successful save state because file persistence completes before async indexing finishes.
   112	
   113	## KEY OUTCOMES
   114	
   115	- Verified successful saves still expose typed post-mutation hooks.
   116	- Verified duplicate no-op saves omit post-mutation hooks and leave caches unchanged.
   117	
   118	<!-- ANCHOR:recovery-hints -->
   119	
   120	## RECOVERY HINTS
   121	
   122	- If this fixture starts failing insufficiency again, add more concrete file, decision, blocker, next action, or outcome evidence instead of weakening the gate.
   123	- Re-run the UX regression suite and the full MCP package test suite after changing the save contract.
   124	
   125	<!-- /ANCHOR:recovery-hints -->
   126

   133	fixture_title: "${title}"
   134	\`\`\`
   135	
   136	<!-- /ANCHOR:metadata -->
   137	`;
   138	}

   149	function cleanupFixtureRows(): void {
   150	  const db = vectorIndex.getDb();
   151	  if (!db) {
   152	    return;
   153	  }
   154	
   155	  const likePattern = '%999-memory-save-ux-fixtures%';
   156	  db.prepare(`DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id IN (
   157	    SELECT id FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE ? OR spec_folder LIKE ?
   158	  )`).run(likePattern, likePattern);
   159	  db.prepare(`DELETE FROM memory_lineage WHERE memory_id IN (
   160	    SELECT id FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE ? OR spec_folder LIKE ?
   161	  )`).run(likePattern, likePattern);
   162	  db.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_conflicts WHERE spec_folder LIKE ?').run(likePattern);
   163	  // Delete history rows before memory_index to satisfy FK constraint
   164	  db.prepare(`DELETE FROM memory_history WHERE memory_id IN (
   165	    SELECT id FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE ? OR spec_folder LIKE ?
   166	  )`).run(likePattern, likePattern);
   167	  db.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE file_path LIKE ? OR spec_folder LIKE ?').run(likePattern, likePattern);
   168	}

One useful wrinkle is emerging from the external repo: its test suite is strongest at pure checker units and parsing rules, not full repair-loop or CLI end-to-end coverage. I’m checking that directly so I can separate what we should copy from what we should improve on in Spec Kit.

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"describe\\(|it\\(\" .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/*.ts" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
   330	| **Causal Graph** | Follows cause-and-effect links between memories     | "Why did we choose this?" questions            |
   331	| **Degree**       | Scores by graph connectivity, weighted by edge type | Finding important hub decisions                |
   332	
   333	Results from all channels are combined using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with a K parameter tuned per query intent. A memory that scores well in multiple channels rises to the top.
   334	
   335	#### Search Pipeline
   336	
   337	Every search goes through four stages, like an assembly line where each station has one clear job:
   338	
   339	1. **Gather** -- collect candidates from active channels in parallel. Constitutional memories are always injected regardless of score.
   340	2. **Score** -- fuse channel results with RRF, then apply 8 post-fusion scoring signals (co-activation boost, FSRS decay, interference penalty, cold-start boost, session recency, causal 2-hop, intent weights, channel min-representation).
   341	3. **Rerank** -- run a local cross-encoder model to re-check ranking accuracy. Chunks are collapsed back to parent memories. If your machine lacks VRAM, the reranker gracefully skips.
   342	4. **Filter** -- enforce score immutability, apply state filtering, annotate with confidence labels (high/medium/low) and truncate at the confidence gap.
   343	
   344	#### Query Intelligence
   345	
   346	Before any search runs, the system figures out what kind of help you need -- like a triage nurse who reads your symptoms and routes you to the right specialist.
   347	
   348	- **Complexity routing** sizes up your question and picks how many channels to use (2 for simple, 4 for moderate, all 5 for complex)
   349	- **Intent classification** maps your query to one of 7 task types (add_feature, fix_bug, refactor, security_audit, understand, find_spec, find_decision), each with its own channel weight profile
   350	- **Query decomposition** splits multi-topic questions into focused sub-queries without needing an LLM call
   351	- **HyDE fallback** writes a hypothetical answer to your question, then searches for real documents matching it -- surfaces content your original wording missed
   352	
   353	#### Memory Lifecycle
   354	
   355	Not all memories are equally useful forever. The system uses FSRS (Free Spaced Repetition Scheduler) to track freshness -- a decay model validated on 100M+ Anki flashcard users.
   356	
   357	| Tier               | Description                       | Decay Behavior                           |
   358	| ------------------ | --------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
   359	| **Constitutional** | Always-surface rules (3.0x boost) | Never decays                             |
   360	| **Critical**       | High-importance decisions         | Never decays or decays at 2x slower rate |
   361	| **Important**      | Significant patterns              | 1.5x slower than normal                  |
   362	| **Normal**         | Standard session context          | Standard FSRS decay                      |
   363	| **Temporary**      | Quick scratch notes               | Fast decay                               |
   364	| **Deprecated**     | Superseded content                | Fastest decay                            |
   365	
   366	Decay speed is also controlled by content type (decisions decay slower than general notes). Memories earn promotions through positive feedback: 5 thumbs-up promotes normal to important, 10 promotes to critical.
   367	
   368	Five cognitive states track access patterns: **HOT** (just used) through **WARM**, **COLD**, **DORMANT** to **ARCHIVED**. Hot memories get full content in results. Warm ones appear as summaries. Cold and below only surface if they score well enough.
   369	
   370	#### Causal Graph
   371	
   372	The system tracks how decisions relate to each other -- like a corkboard with sticky notes connected by string. One note says "we chose JWT tokens." A string connects it to "because the session store was too slow." Another string connects that to "the Redis outage on March 5th."
   373	
   374	Six relationship types: `caused`, `enabled`, `supersedes`, `contradicts`, `derived_from`, `supports`. Community detection (Louvain algorithm) automatically clusters related memories so finding one surfaces its neighbors.
   375	
   376	#### Save Intelligence
   377	
   378	When you save new content, the system runs an arbitration process before storing anything. Prediction Error gating compares incoming content against existing memories and picks one of four outcomes:
   379	
   380	| Outcome       | When                               | What Happens                                |
   381	| ------------- | ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
   382	| **CREATE**    | Nothing similar exists             | Stored as new knowledge                     |
   383	| **REINFORCE** | Similar exists, new one adds value | Both kept, existing gets a confidence boost |
   384	| **UPDATE**    | Similar exists, new one is better  | Old version replaced in place               |
   385	| **SUPERSEDE** | New knowledge contradicts the old  | New version active, old one demoted         |
   386	
   387	Three quality gates run before storage: structure check (required format and metadata), semantic sufficiency check (enough real content to be useful), and duplicate detection.
   388	Short decision-type memories can bypass the content-length gate when SPECKIT_SAVE_QUALITY_GATE_EXCEPTIONS=true and at least two structural signals (title, specFolder, or anchor) are present.
   389	
   390	#### Shared Memory
   391	
   392	By default, every memory is private. Shared memory adds controlled access so teams and multi-agent setups can share a knowledge pool.
   393	
   394	Think of it like a shared office with a keycard lock. The office stays locked until an admin activates it. Only people on the access list can enter. Management can lock it down instantly if something goes wrong.
   395	
   396	- **Deny-by-default** -- nobody gets access unless explicitly granted
   397	- **Three roles** -- `owner` (full control), `editor` (read/write), `viewer` (read-only)
   398	- **Kill switch** -- immediately blocks all reads for emergencies
   399	
   400	For the full shared memory guide, see [`SHARED_MEMORY_DATABASE.md`](./SHARED_MEMORY_DATABASE.md).
   401	
   402	#### Evaluation Infrastructure
   403	
   404	The memory system includes built-in tools for measuring search quality:
   405	
   406	- **Ablation studies** -- turn off one search component at a time to measure its contribution, like removing one ingredient from a recipe to see if the dish still tastes good
   407	- **12-metric computation** -- MRR, NDCG, MAP and 9 other information retrieval metrics
   408	- **Synthetic ground truth corpus** -- 110 test questions with known correct answers for benchmarking, keyed to live parent-memory IDs; rerun `scripts/evals/map-ground-truth-ids.ts` after DB rebuilds or imports before trusting ablation or reporting comparisons
   409	- **Reporting dashboard** -- performance trends across work periods and search channels, sourced from parent-memory-normalized eval rows even when retrieval hits came from chunks
   410	
   411	---
   412	
   413	<!-- divider:4.3 -->
   414	
   415	### 3.3 COMMANDS
   416	
   417	Spec Kit exposes 12 top-level workflow commands: 8 `spec_kit` + 4 `memory` operations. Repository-wide command entry points total 21 when combined with 6 `create` commands, 2 `improve` commands, and 1 `agent_router` utility. Each command opens access to a specific set of tools.
   418	
   419	#### Spec Kit Commands (8)
   420
