# Iteration 033: TESTING STRATEGY

## Focus
TESTING STRATEGY: How should we test the adopted patterns? Unit tests, integration tests, memory quality regression tests. Concrete test plans.

## Findings
### Finding 1: Mirror Mex’s checker tests for the adopt-now integrity lane, but keep them as pure unit suites
- **Source**: [external/test/checkers.test.ts#L40](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/checkers.test.ts#L40), [external/test/checkers.test.ts#L122](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/checkers.test.ts#L122), [external/test/checkers.test.ts#L260](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/checkers.test.ts#L260)
- **What it does**: Mex uses temp-dir fixtures to test each checker in isolation, including severity downgrades, scaffold-root resolution, and comment/INDEX edge cases.
- **Why it matters**: Our adopted `path` / `edges` / `index-sync` slice should be tested without DB, embeddings, or retrieval noise. The fastest stable plan is one fixture-driven unit suite per checker plus a shared fixture helper.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 2: Add claim-extraction boundary tests before checker tests, because false positives will be the main migration risk
- **Source**: [external/test/claims.test.ts#L23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/claims.test.ts#L23), [external/test/claims.test.ts#L47](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/claims.test.ts#L47), [external/test/claims.test.ts#L120](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/claims.test.ts#L120)
- **What it does**: Mex tests placeholder skipping, route-vs-file discrimination, code-snippet filtering, wildcard exclusion, bare filename extraction, and negated-section marking before checker execution.
- **Why it matters**: For Spec Kit, integrity failures will only be trusted if extraction avoids noisy matches from commands, examples, placeholders, or intentional “does not exist” prose. This is the calibration layer.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 3: The save and maintenance surface needs contract-style integration tests, not just unit tests
- **Source**: [generate-context-cli-authority.vitest.ts#L55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/generate-context-cli-authority.vitest.ts#L55), [memory-save-pipeline-enforcement.vitest.ts#L61](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-save-pipeline-enforcement.vitest.ts#L61), [memory-save-ux-regressions.vitest.ts#L221](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-save-ux-regressions.vitest.ts#L221), [README.md#L376](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/README.md#L376)
- **What it does**: Spec Kit already tests CLI authority, multi-gate save enforcement, duplicate no-op behavior, deferred embedding UX, and the documented three-gate save pipeline.
- **Why it matters**: The adopted maintenance surface and any future `spec-kit doctor` flow will live or die on handoff contracts: target resolution, dry-run vs write behavior, confirmation-only safety, and exact response envelopes.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 4: Retrieval quality must stay in a separate regression lane with evals, not be folded into integrity tests
- **Source**: [ablation-framework.vitest.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/ablation-framework.vitest.ts#L1), [memory-search-eval-channels.vitest.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-search-eval-channels.vitest.ts#L1), [learned-feedback.vitest.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/learned-feedback.vitest.ts#L1), [README.md#L402](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/README.md#L402)
- **What it does**: The repo already has ablation tests, per-channel eval logging, and learned-feedback safeguard tests around search quality and ranking behavior.
- **Why it matters**: The Mex-style integrity lane only tells us whether markdown claims match disk. It does not tell us whether `memory_search`, `memory_context`, or trigger routing got better or worse. That needs its own benchmark corpus and regression thresholds.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high
- **Source strength**: primary

### Finding 5: Future `spec-kit doctor` and freshness work should be tested as lifecycle/state-transition flows, not score snapshots
- **Source**: [handler-memory-health-edge.vitest.ts#L39](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/handler-memory-health-edge.vitest.ts#L39), [handler-memory-context.vitest.ts#L117](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/handler-memory-context.vitest.ts#L117), [code-graph-scan.vitest.ts#L50](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-scan.vitest.ts#L50), [graph-lifecycle.vitest.ts#L123](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/graph-lifecycle.vitest.ts#L123), [external/src/drift/scoring.ts#L3](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/src/drift/scoring.ts#L3)
- **What it does**: Our tests already assert confirmation-only health behavior, context-mode routing, graph reindex transitions, and refresh flag defaults. Mex’s score model is just arithmetic.
- **Why it matters**: For us, the valuable behavior is “did the system choose the safe next step and avoid unintended mutation,” not “did the score change by N points.” Use state-machine tests for health, freshness, routing, and recheck loops; reject score-only acceptance.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: medium
- **Source strength**: primary

## Sources Consulted
- [external/README.md#L72](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/README.md#L72)
- [external/package.json#L36](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/package.json#L36)
- [external/test/checkers.test.ts#L40](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/checkers.test.ts#L40)
- [external/test/claims.test.ts#L23](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/claims.test.ts#L23)
- [external/test/scoring.test.ts#L15](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/002-mex-main/external/test/scoring.test.ts#L15)
- [generate-context-cli-authority.vitest.ts#L55](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/generate-context-cli-authority.vitest.ts#L55)
- [memory-save-pipeline-enforcement.vitest.ts#L61](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-save-pipeline-enforcement.vitest.ts#L61)
- [memory-save-ux-regressions.vitest.ts#L221](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-save-ux-regressions.vitest.ts#L221)
- [ablation-framework.vitest.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/ablation-framework.vitest.ts#L1)
- [memory-search-eval-channels.vitest.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/memory-search-eval-channels.vitest.ts#L1)
- [learned-feedback.vitest.ts#L1](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/learned-feedback.vitest.ts#L1)
- [handler-memory-health-edge.vitest.ts#L39](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/handler-memory-health-edge.vitest.ts#L39)

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.32
- Questions addressed: how to unit-test the integrity lane; how to integration-test the maintenance/save surface; how to regression-test search quality; how to test future doctor/freshness flows
- Questions answered: unit tests should target claim extraction plus pure integrity checkers; integration tests should cover `generate-context`, `memory_save`, `memory_health`, `memory_context`, and graph refresh contracts; quality regressions should use ablation plus per-channel logging and learned-feedback safeguards; `spec-kit doctor` should be tested as a lifecycle flow, not a score
- Novelty justification: prior iterations selected the patterns, but this pass converts them into a concrete three-lane test strategy with existing suite anchors and acceptance boundaries

## Ruled Out
- Using Mex’s `100 - 10/error - 3/warning - 1/info` score as the main release gate for Spec Kit
- Treating integrity tests as a proxy for retrieval quality
- Copying Mex’s mostly unit-focused coverage ceiling without adding contract and lifecycle integration tests

## Reflection
- What worked: reading the external and local test suites directly was the fastest route, because the testing strategy is encoded more clearly in tests than in README prose
- What did not work: the CocoIndex search attempt was canceled, so this iteration relied on direct file inspection only
- What I would do differently: next pass, map the proposed integrity fixtures to exact handler filenames before implementation so the first test PR can be scoped mechanically

## Recommended Next Focus
Implement the test plan in three lanes.

1. `integrity-unit`
Create fixture-driven suites for claim extraction and the adopt-now checkers.
Pass bar: every seeded placeholder, negated section, comment, scaffold-root, and orphan/missing case returns the expected issue code and severity.

2. `contract-integration`
Add or extend tests around `generate-context` authority, `memory_save` dry-run/write/no-op UX, `memory_health` confirmation-only behavior, `memory_context` routing, and `code_graph_scan` stale/full-reindex transitions.
Pass bar: exact response envelopes are stable, and non-mutating flows do not write.

3. `quality-regression`
Keep a fixed benchmark lane for `memory_search`, `memory_context`, and trigger retrieval using ablation, per-channel eval logging, and learned-feedback safeguards.
Pass bar: no unintended drop beyond agreed thresholds such as `Recall@20` or `MRR`, and channel-attribution logs remain complete.

Read-only note: strict phase validation passed for `002-mex-main` with `Errors: 0  Warnings: 0`, but I could not persist `iteration-033.md`, update `research.md`, create `implementation-summary.md`, or run `generate-context.js` in this sandbox.
